summary: A rough audience tape of the July 12 1970 Fillmore East gig captures a spectacular, Pigpen‑heavy set with standout moments like “Attics of My Life,” “Turn On Your Lovelight” and an upbeat Tight Up jam.
review: The recording is an audience capture that suffers from occasional muffiness, tape‑speed quirks and drop‑outs, but it still conveys the raw energy of a midnight‑to‑dawn show. Musically the concert shines – the acoustic Pigpen set, the New Riders interlude, and electric marathon are repeatedly praised, especially “Attics of My Life,” Pigpen’s vocal showcases and a lively Tight Up jam.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (10+) give five‑star ratings and highlight the show’s musical greatness; recording flaws are noted but not enough to outweigh the performance, yielding a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Hand claps, occasional muffled sections and tape‑speed slowdown make parts hard to hear; overall listenable with EQ/headphone boost recommended.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Midnight acoustic Pigpen set, followed by New Riders with Garcia on steel guitar, then the electric Dead taking over around 3 am; the marathon show builds to a joyous climax and ends at sunrise.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s vocals and his steel‑guitar work with the New Riders are praised; his overall presence is noted as “dosed” but energetic.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ/vocals receive glowing remarks, especially on “It's a Man's World” and “Turn On Your Lovelight”.
